sections.heading: What is Retrofit?
sections.content: Retrofit is a type-safe HTTP client for Android developed by Square. It allows developers to convert REST API endpoints into Kotlin interface functions using annotations. Instead of manually building HTTP requests and parsing responses, you simply define an API interface and Retrofit handles the network request, JSON parsing, and threading automatically. Retrofit works together with OkHttp for network communication and uses converters such as Gson or Moshi to transform JSON responses into Kotlin data classes.

sections.heading: Why Use Retrofit?
items: Type-safe API calls using Kotlin interfaces
Simple annotation-based endpoint definition
Automatic JSON parsing using converters like Gson or Moshi
Built-in coroutine support using suspend functions
Uses OkHttp internally for efficient networking
Reduces networking boilerplate code significantly

sections.heading: Basic Retrofit Setup
sections.content: To start using Retrofit, you must add the Retrofit dependency and a JSON converter to your project. Retrofit requires a base URL and an API interface that defines the endpoints. Each endpoint is represented by a function annotated with HTTP methods such as GET or POST.

sections.heading: Basic Setup Steps
items: Add Retrofit dependency to your project
Add a JSON converter such as Gson
Add INTERNET permission in AndroidManifest
Create Kotlin data classes matching the API response
Define an API interface with Retrofit annotations
Create a Retrofit instance using Retrofit.Builder()

rows.description: Creates a new resource on the server
rows.example: @POST("users") suspend fun createUser(@Body user: User): User
@PUT("users/{id}") suspend fun updateUser(@Path("id") id: Int, @Body user: User)

sections.heading: What is OkHttp?
sections.content: OkHttp is the HTTP client used internally by Retrofit to perform network requests. It manages connections, handles caching, retries failed requests, and provides powerful interceptors that allow developers to inspect or modify requests and responses.

sections.heading: Key Features of OkHttp
items: Connection pooling for efficient network usage
Built-in caching support
Automatic retries and redirects
Request and response interceptors
Configurable timeouts for network operations

sections.heading: OkHttp Interceptors
sections.content: Interceptors allow developers to intercept and modify network requests and responses. They are commonly used to add authentication headers, log requests, or handle errors globally.

sections.heading: Common Interceptor Uses
items: Adding Authorization headers for authenticated APIs
Logging request and response data during development
Handling token refresh logic
Monitoring or modifying network traffic

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: Retrofit simplifies Android networking by converting REST API endpoints into Kotlin interface functions using annotations. It automatically handles HTTP requests, JSON parsing, and threading while relying on OkHttp as the underlying network client. OkHttp provides powerful features such as interceptors, caching, and connection management. Together, Retrofit and OkHttp form the standard networking stack used in modern Android applications.
